request.concept: An AI assistant for boutique agencies that turns messy meeting notes into client-ready summaries, follow-up emails, and internal task plans.
request.additional_context: Optimize for time-to-value within one session.

input.concept: An AI assistant for boutique agencies that turns messy meeting notes into client-ready summaries, follow-up emails, and internal task plans.
input.additional_context: Optimize for time-to-value within one session.

product_brief.target_user: Small agencies that need to move faster without growing headcount
product_brief.core_problem: Account teams lose time converting rough meeting notes into aligned client follow-up and internal delivery work.
product_brief.solution_summary: SignalBrief converts messy notes into structured summaries, follow-up drafts, and internal action plans that teams can review before sending.
product_brief.differentiator: It is optimized for agency handoff workflows instead of acting like a general-purpose note summarizer.

user_journey_loops.name: Capture meeting context
user_journey_loops.objective: Turn raw notes into a usable draft without cleanup-heavy onboarding.
user_steps: Select a client and desired output type
user_journey_loops.success_signal: The first draft is usable enough that the team edits instead of rewriting from scratch.

user_journey_loops.name: Approve the follow-up plan
user_journey_loops.objective: Review and refine the draft into an internal or client-ready deliverable.
user_steps: Adjust owners, due dates, and wording
Export or copy the final version
user_journey_loops.success_signal: The user sends or saves a final plan in the same session.

user_journey_loops.name: Reuse successful templates
user_journey_loops.objective: Encourage repeat usage across multiple client accounts.
user_steps: Reuse the same structure on the next meeting
user_journey_loops.success_signal: Teams come back for another meeting workflow within the same week.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Price against visible time saved
monetization_risk_notes.note: Agencies will pay when the product clearly reduces coordination time across client follow-up work.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Tie paid plans to seats, saved templates, and shared workflows rather than raw AI usage.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Scope creep is the main execution risk
monetization_risk_notes.note: Calendar sync, CRM sync, and PM integrations could overwhelm the first release.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Start with one copy-paste workflow and a single export target.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Retention depends on repeat meetings
monetization_risk_notes.note: The product is less sticky if it is useful only for one-off note cleanup.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Focus early UX on recurring client-account workflows and reusable templates.

monetization_risk_notes.title: Positioning must be sharper than 'AI assistant'
monetization_risk_notes.note: Agencies already see many generic note tools and need a workflow-specific reason to switch.
monetization_risk_notes.mitigation: Lead with agency delivery speed, client follow-up clarity, and reduced admin overhead.

task_list.title: Define the single agency workflow the product must solve first
task_list.rationale: A focused workflow makes prompt design, positioning, and validation much easier.

task_list.title: Write the structured output schema and critique rubric
task_list.rationale: The schema is the contract between prompting, evaluation, and downstream UI behavior.

task_list.title: Build a vertical slice from note input to editable JSON
task_list.rationale: The product needs one complete path before integrations or collaboration features matter.

task_list.title: Pilot the workflow with five agencies
task_list.rationale: The team needs evidence that outputs are decision-ready for real client work.

task_list.title: Decide pricing and packaging for the first paid tier
task_list.rationale: Business model decisions affect saved history, template sharing, and seat management.

follow_up_questions.question: Is the first buyer more likely to be the agency owner, ops lead, or account manager?
follow_up_questions.reason: This changes both packaging and onboarding design.

follow_up_questions.question: Which output has to feel almost instant for the product to seem worth trying?
follow_up_questions.reason: The answer clarifies the most important first-session quality bar.

follow_up_questions.question: Will early adopters expect task exports before they are willing to adopt the workflow?
follow_up_questions.reason: This affects whether integrations belong in the MVP.

recommended_revisions: Validate whether agency owners or account leads are the first buyers before expanding collaboration features.
